title: ZimerfeldTree - Restore
informHashAndFile: Enter the commit hash and the file path.
informHashOrRange: Enter the commit hash or range.
confirmHardWarn:
WARNING: git reset --hard will discard ALL uncommitted files in '{0}'.

This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
confirmResetTagWarn:
WARNING: git reset --hard will move the branch '{0}' to the tag '{1}' and DISCARD all later commits and changes.

This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Do you want to continue?
aboutBody:
Buttons:

  Emergency Plan
    Restores or resets a branch to the state of a tag (release).
    Restore to Tag — git checkout <tag> -- .
      Brings the tag's content into the branch as staged changes; history untouched.
    Reset to Tag — git reset --hard <tag>
      Moves the branch pointer to the tag and DISCARDS later commits/changes.
      IRREVERSIBLE action, asks for confirmation.

  Restore File
    Recovers a specific file from the state of an old commit.
    Equivalent to: git checkout <hash> -- <file>
    The changes are left staged, ready to commit.

  Cherry-Pick
    Applies one or more commits onto the current branch.
    Equivalent to: git cherry-pick <hash>
    For a range use: <old-hash>..<recent-hash>

  Reset Branch
    Moves the branch pointer to an earlier commit.
    --mixed  Undoes commits, keeps changes as unstaged (default).
    --soft   Undoes commits, keeps changes as staged.
    --hard   Undoes commits and DISCARDS all local changes.

How to find a commit hash:

  git log --oneline             lists every commit from HEAD
  git log --oneline -20         last 20 commits
  git log --oneline <branch>    commits of a specific branch
  git log --oneline A..B        commits in B that are not in A
  git log --oneline --all       commits of every branch

The hash dropdowns show the HEAD of each local branch.
You can select a branch or type any hash manually.
